name: Margaret Berestyuk
focus: Landscape archaeology • Environmental change • Geophysical survey analysis
bio: Margaret E. Berestyuk is a graduate student in Anthropology at Northern Arizona University whose research focuses on archaeology, landscape analysis, and environmental change. Her work explores how past human populations interacted with and adapted to their environments, with interests in landscape archaeology, cultural resource management, and climate-related impacts on archaeological sites. She has contributed to field and research projects involving geophysical survey methods and excavation, including work analyzing spatial patterns and subsurface features at archaeological sites. Berestyuk’s research integrates geospatial analysis, environmental data, and archaeological methods to better understand long-term human-environment relationships.
